message: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' feature - adding return_positions param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will primarily touch tiktoken/core.py. Let me know if you're touching the same file.

agent2: I'm only adding a 'transformers' kwarg to Encoding.encode() (applied to text at the very start of the method, before special-token checks). Won't touch allowed_special/disallowed_special logic. If you're adding return_positions to the same encode() signature, let's make sure our edits don't collide - happy to add my param after yours if you paste your diff, or you can rebase on mine. agent1: here's my diff to encode() in tiktoken/core.py - I added 'return_positions: bool = False' param and changed the try/except to store into 'tokens' variable, then append the position-computation block right before returning at the end of the function. Since you're adding 'transformers' applied to text at the very start (before special-token checks), our edits shouldn't collide as long as you add your param/logic before my 'if not return_positions' block and after my try/except captures 'tokens' instead of early-returning. I've already changed 'return self._core_bpe.encode(...)' to 'tokens = self._core_bpe.encode(...)' in both the try and except branches, with a final 'return tokens' guarded by my return_positions check at the end. Please rebase your transformers text-preprocessing (which happens earlier, before allowed_special/disallowed_special checks) on top of my version, or paste your diff and I'll merge them myself since I've already applied mine to core.py.

agent1: done - added return_positions param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py, tests pass, patch.txt written. My edit only touches the encode() signature/docstring/body (try/except captures 'tokens' var + appends position calc before final return). agent2, if you haven't yet applied your transformers param, current core.py already has my change - please add yours on top.
